name: shared-patterns
description: Shared prompt patterns for all workflow phases - todo management, approval loops, agent dispatch

sections.todo_management:
## Todo Management

For phases with multiple items to process:

### Creating Todos
- Create a **separate todo for EACH item** (character, setting, scene)
- Format: "[Action]: [Item Name]" (e.g., "Create character profile: Alice")
- **NEVER** create a single todo for "all characters" or "all items"

### Todo States
- `pending` - Not started yet
- `in_progress` - Currently working on this item (only ONE at a time)
- `completed` - Item approved and saved

### Workflow
1. Create all todos upfront for known items
2. Mark ONE todo as `in_progress` before working on it
3. Complete that item (generate, get approval, save)
4. Mark todo as `completed`
5. Move to next todo
sections.approval_loop:
## Approval Loop

For each item requiring approval:

### Standard Flow
1. Generate content using appropriate agent (dispatch_content_agent, dispatch_image_agent, dispatch_video_agent)
2. Present result to user using ask_user
3. Handle response:
   - **Approved**: Update item status, save artifact ID, mark todo completed
   - **Rejected with feedback**: Store feedback, regenerate with feedback
   - **No response (15s)**: Auto-approve and proceed

### Project Updates After Approval
```
// For characters:
update_project(action: "update_character_approval", data: {
  name: "[Name]",
  status: "approved",
  contentArtifactId: "[artifact-id]"
})

// For settings:
update_project(action: "update_setting_approval", data: {
  name: "[Name]",
  status: "approved",
  contentArtifactId: "[artifact-id]"
})

// For scenes:
update_project(action: "update_scene_approval", data: {
  scene_number: N,
  approval_type: "content" | "image" | "video",
  status: "approved",
  artifactId: "[artifact-id]"
})
```
sections.agent_dispatch:
## Agent Dispatch

### Content Agent (for descriptions)
```
dispatch_content_agent(
  task: "Create [type] description for [name]",
  content_type: "character" | "setting" | "scene",
  context_ref: "[stored-context-id]"  // Use for large content
)
```

### Image Agent (for image generation)
```
dispatch_image_agent(
  task: "Generate [type] image for [name]",
  prompt: "[detailed visual description]",
  style: "[style notes]",
  reference_image_ids: ["[ref-id-1]", "[ref-id-2]"]  // For consistency
)
```

### Video Agent (for video generation)
```
dispatch_video_agent(
  task: "Generate video for scene [N]",
  scene_image_artifact_id: "[image-artifact-id]",
  scene_number: N,
  motion_description: "[motion/camera description]"
)
```
sections.phase_completion:
## Phase Completion

When all items in a per-item phase are approved:

1. Verify: Check that `areAllItemsApproved` returns true (shown in next_action)
2. Mark phase complete:
   ```
   update_project(action: "update_planner_stage", data: {
     phase: "[current_phase]",
     stage: "complete"
   })
   ```
   This automatically marks the phase status as "completed"
3. Transition to next phase:
   ```
   update_project(action: "transition_phase", data: {})
   ```
sections.error_handling:
## Error Handling

### Generation Failures
- Log the error
- Increment regeneration count for the item
- Offer to retry with adjusted parameters
- DO NOT mark item as approved if generation failed

### Max Regenerations
- If an item has been regenerated 3+ times, ask user for guidance
- Consider simplifying the request or skipping the item
sections.context_management:
## Context Management

For large content that needs to be passed to sub-agents:

### Storing Context
```
store_context(
  variable_name: "$chapter_1",
  label: "Chapter 1 content",
  content: "[large text content]"
)
```

### Using Stored Context
When dispatching agents, use `context_ref` instead of inline content:
```
dispatch_content_agent(
  task: "...",
  content_type: "...",
  context_ref: "$chapter_1"  // References stored content
)
```

This preserves full content without summarization.

metadata.description: These patterns are common across all workflow phases and should be referenced in phase-specific prompts
